food_subgroup: Chinese rice bowl and bento bundle set; multi-component meal for two persons comprising steamed rice, protein dishes, and side vegetables

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a multi-component Chinese rice bowl bundle set. FOODON_00002473 (meal food product) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FJP (composite food - mixed dish) is the closest parent for a multi-component Chinese rice bowl bento bundle. No exact FoodEx2 code exists for this specific restaurant bundle format.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for a restaurant bundle meal set of this type.

main_ingredients: protein (chicken, pork, or tofu depending on selection)

serving.description: 1 bundle set for 2 persons (approximately 2 rice bowls with protein and sides)
serving.natural_language_portion: One bundle set for two persons, comprising approximately two portions of steamed rice (approximately 200 g each) with protein dishes and vegetable sides, totalling an estimated 700 g combined weight.

nutrient_source.title: HPB Singapore Nutrient Composition Database — Steamed White Rice with Mixed Dishes (Chinese-style rice bowl composite estimate)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists in HPB Foodo for a '2 Pax Premium Bundle Set'. Nutrient values are estimated by compositing HPB Foodo data for steamed white rice (per 100 g cooked), braised/stir-fried protein dishes (chicken, pork), and stir-fried vegetables typical of a Chinese rice kitchen bento. The composite reflects a representative mixed Chinese rice bowl meal. Values are expressed per 100 g of the combined edible portion. USDA SR Legacy used as secondary cross-check for individual ingredient macros.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Steamed white rice (Chinese-style) and mixed rice dish analogues
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a '2 Pax Premium Bundle Set'. GI of 65 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cooked white rice (Chinese-style, GI approximately 64–72, median ~65) as the dominant carbohydrate contributor. The mixed protein and vegetable components lower the effective meal GI slightly; however, white rice dominates the glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 37.1) / 100 ≈ 24.1, based on estimated 185 g carbohydrate-contributing portion (approximately 200 g cooked rice × 2 servings × 18.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g = 37.1 g net carbohydrate per serving for one person; bundle GL reported for one person's share).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 380 mg per 100 g; a full bundle serving for one person may contribute 530–760 mg sodium depending on portion size and sauce quantity
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sauce or sauce-on-the-side options and limit portion size to one rice bowl to keep sodium intake within the recommended 2000 mg daily limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Dominant carbohydrate source is white rice (GI ~65, medium), with a glycemic load of approximately 24 per person's share of the bundle
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider substituting white rice with brown rice or reducing rice portion to half, and pairing with higher-protein and higher-fibre side dishes to blunt the postprandial glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat approximately 1.4 g per 100 g; sodium approximately 380 mg per 100 g; overall fat profile is moderate with some saturated fat from protein dishes and cooking oils
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should choose lean protein options (steamed chicken or tofu) over braised pork, request less sauce to reduce sodium and saturated fat, and ensure vegetable sides are included to increase dietary fibre intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The 2 Pax Premium Bundle Set is a Chinese-style rice kitchen meal bundle designed for two persons, served at First Bowl Rice Kitchen in Singapore. It is a multi-component bento-style set comprising steamed white rice, a selection of protein dishes (typically braised or stir-fried chicken, pork, or tofu), and stir-fried or blanched vegetable sides, all served with savoury sauce or gravy. The meal reflects the Singaporean Chinese cai fan (菜饭) tradition, where diners select from a variety of cooked dishes to accompany steamed rice. The rice is soft and fluffy, the protein dishes are tender and flavourful with soy-based or oyster sauce seasonings, and the vegetables provide a mild, slightly sweet contrast. Nutritionally, the bundle is a balanced mixed meal providing moderate protein (approximately 8.5 g per 100 g), moderate carbohydrate from white rice (approximately 18.5 g per 100 g), and moderate fat (approximately 4.8 g per 100 g). Sodium content is notable at approximately 380 mg per 100 g due to sauces and seasonings. The glycaemic index is approximately 65 (medium), driven primarily by white rice. Clinically, the meal is suitable for most healthy adults but requires portion and sauce moderation for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The bundle format makes it a practical shared meal option for families or colleagues dining together in a casual Singaporean Chinese food court or restaurant setting.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists in HPB Foodo or any approved primary source for a '2 Pax Premium Bundle Set' from First Bowl Rice Kitchen. Nutrient values are estimated by compositing HPB Foodo data for steamed white rice and typical Chinese-style protein and vegetable dishes. The composite is representative of the food category but cannot be verified against a specific restaurant recipe. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported in HPB Foodo for composite Chinese rice bowl dishes and is not a primary nutrient of concern for this food category. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source for this food type. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in typical Singaporean Chinese home-style cooking using vegetable oils per HPB Foodo composite data. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for cooked white rice (Chinese-style); the bundle has no direct GI measurement. Nutrient values represent a per-100 g estimate of the combined edible portion of the full bundle (rice, protein, vegetables, sauce). The bundle is served by First Bowl Rice Kitchen at Funan, Singapore, priced at SGD 24.90. Allergen profile is indicative only; actual allergens depend on specific dish selection within the bundle. Halal status is not confirmed for this restaurant.
